plan.title: feat(scope): task scope:undo (single + batch-id reversibility) (#1134 / D15)

narratives.Description: D15 introduces `task scope:undo` -- the generic reversibility verb for scope-lifecycle audit entries on `vbrief/.eval/scope-lifecycle.jsonl`. Two modes ship in one PR: (1) `task scope:undo <decision_id>` reverses a single audit entry (`demote` -> re-promote, `cancel` -> restore-from-cancelled-to-prior-folder, `restore` -> re-cancel); terminal actions `complete` / `fail` are explicitly REFUSED. (2) `task scope:undo --batch-id=<uuid>` reverses every entry tagged with the given batch-id, idempotent on already-undone entries. Each undo writes a fresh `action: 'undo'` audit entry with `original_decision_id`; batch undos are themselves tagged with a new `undo-batch-id` so the undo cohort is also reversible. `--dry-run` previews without writes; `--decision-id` is mutually exclusive with `--batch-id`; positional `<decision_id>` is shorthand for `--decision-id`. As a small adjacent fix, `scripts/scope_demote.batch_demote` now stamps every batch-demote entry with a shared `batch_id` field in `demote_meta` (forward-fill for the umbrella claim in the #1134 body). Out of scope: cross-batch rollback, time-travel snapshot restore, TUI confirmation dialogs, terminal-action reversal.
narratives.Origin: Wave-2d-1 D15 child of umbrella #1119 (audit-log reversibility layer, sibling to D1 / #1121 scope:demote).
narratives.Overview:
## What to build

1. `scripts/scope_undo.py` -- single + batch-id undo driver (mirrors `scripts/scope_demote.py` shape).
2. `scripts/scope_demote.py` -- add `batch_id` to `demote_meta` written by `batch_demote` (minor forward-fill).
3. `tasks/scope-undo.yml` -- new fragment exposing `undo` (per the post-D12 sub-task convention).
4. `Taskfile.yml` -- include the new fragment + root alias `scope:undo`.
5. `skills/deft-directive-refinement/SKILL.md` -- Phase 4 verb table updated.
6. Tests under `tests/cli/test_scope_undo.py` covering demote-undo round-trip, cancel-undo round-trip, batch undo on a 5-entry cohort, idempotency, terminal-action refusal, --dry-run output, --batch-id / --decision-id mutex.

## Acceptance criteria

- `task scope:undo <decision_id>` reverses a single demote / cancel / restore audit entry.
- `task scope:undo --batch-id=<uuid>` reverses every entry in the batch (idempotent on already-undone).
- Terminal actions (`complete`, `fail`) refused with exit 1 and a clear error.
- `--dry-run` previews the cohort without writes; exit 0.
- Each undo writes an `action: 'undo'` audit entry with `original_decision_id`; batch undos carry `undo_batch_id`.
- `task check` green (lint + test + encoding).
- PR body references #1134 + #1119 + #1121 + #845.

## Type

Feature -- framework scope-lifecycle reversibility primitive; consumer-agnostic.

## Refs

- Umbrella: https://github.com/deftai/directive/issues/1119
- Issue: https://github.com/deftai/directive/issues/1134
- Companion: D1 / #1121 (`task scope:demote` + audit-log surface this consumes).

references.title: Issue #1121: D1 -- task scope:demote (audit-log surface consumed)
